title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

title: Blood and lymphatic vessels connect cancer, heart disease and neurodegeneration
content:
Blood and lymphatic vessels connect cancer, heart disease and neurodegeneration

lindymar@helsinki.fi Fri, 08/05/2026 - 14:19
Michael Jeltsch, Professor in Pharmaceutical Protein Drug Research, studies how blood and lymphatic vessels are regulated in the human body. His research has direct relevance to cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative diseases, and he is currently developing a new promising drug candidate.

content:

sxsilven@helsinki.fi Fri, 08/05/2026 - 09:48
The HIKET workshop will lay the groundwork for closer collaboration between the two countries and initiate discussions on developing a shared Finnish-Swedish perspective on advancing EU-level carbon balance accounting.

title: Apply for a group leader position in the Neuroscience Center
content:
Apply for a group leader position in the Neuroscience Center

limaiju@helsinki.fi Thu, 07/05/2026 - 18:19
We are inviting applications from outstanding candidates for 2 GROUP LEADER positions focusing on either clinical research in neurology, psychiatry or neurophysiology OR on computational neuroscience and neuro-AI. The call for applications is open until 24.5.2026.

title: Miten lääkeaineet muuntuvat ihmiskehossa ja luonnossa?
content:
How do drugs transform in the human body and in nature?

lindymar@helsinki.fi Thu, 07/05/2026 - 16:14
Professor of drug metabolism Tiina Sikanen studies how drugs are transformed in the human body and in environmental organisms. She develops methods for measuring individual drug metabolism and investigates inter-species differences in drug elimination to develop drugs that are safer for both humans and the environment.

title: Vesivälitteiset taudit kuriin – uusi menetelmä tunnistaa vesistöjen saastumisen lähteen
content:
Keeping waterborne diseases in check – A new method identifies waterway contamination sources

lindymar@helsinki.fi Wed, 06/05/2026 - 14:35
Wastewater and agricultural runoffs are known to contaminate surface waters, but specific sources of contamination often remain unknown. A recently completed doctoral thesis developed a method for tracing contaminants using genetic markers.
